openai.summary: OpenAI still presents GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna as the frontier coding family, with the `gpt-5.6` alias routing to Sol in the public docs. On the Ireland Codex OAuth lane on Monday, July 20, 2026, the explicit family IDs (`gpt-5.6-sol`, `gpt-5.6-terra`, `gpt-5.6-luna`) still return OK, while the previously observed ChatGPT-account base-ID gate on `gpt-5.6` remains in place. The official changelog still centers the July 9, 2026 GPT-5.6 family release rather than a newer Monday model-change entry.

liveProbes.gpt-5.6: 400/not supported when using Codex with a ChatGPT account

anthropic.summary: Anthropic's overview still presents Claude Fable 5 and Claude Sonnet 5 as broadly available, while Claude Mythos 5 remains invitation-only via Project Glasswing. On the Ireland Claude Code subscription lane on Monday, July 20, 2026, `claude-fable-5` and `claude-sonnet-5` still return OK, and `claude-mythos-5` still returns the expected no-access error.

liveProbes.claude-mythos-5: model/no-access error on this Claude Code lane

google.summary: Google's current models page marks `gemini-2.0-flash` and `gemini-2.0-flash-lite` as shut down, and the official release notes say the Gemini 2.0 Flash family is now shut down as of June 1, 2026. On the Ireland AGY lane on Monday, July 20, 2026, both retired IDs still execute but self-identify as Gemini 3.5 Flash, while the live `gemini_local` adapter catalog still advertises the retired IDs. This is now a routed retirement/routing-drift alert rather than an unchanged catalog note.

xai.summary: xAI docs still position Grok 4.5 as the recommended code/chat model, and the public release-notes surface still points to the July 8, 2026 launch entry rather than a newer Monday model change. On the Ireland Grok host on Monday, July 20, 2026, `grok models` reports a live grok.com login with `grok-4.5` as the default and a direct `grok --single ... -m grok-4.5` probe still returns OK, so the Friday, July 17, 2026 EU-availability confirmation stands unchanged.
xai.hostProbe: grok models -> logged in with grok.com; default model grok-4.5; available model grok-4.5

ollama.installedCatalog: `ollama list` returned `gemma3:12b` (8.1 GB, modified 3 weeks ago) and `qwen3:8b` (5.2 GB, modified 3 weeks ago).
ollama.onlineWatchSummary: Monday, July 20, 2026: the local/Ollama leg recovered and the host now shows `gemma3:12b` and `qwen3:8b` installed. The web library pass did not surface a stronger new sub-12B / <=10GB alert than the existing local storm-resilience watch, so no separate local-model radar issue was opened from today's Monday pass.

openRadar.grok-4.5: RESOLVED 2026-07-17: the Ireland Grok host is now authenticated, `grok models` lists `grok-4.5`, and `grok --single -m grok-4.5` returns OK. The parked EU-watch should now hand off to a real Grok 4.5 bench.
openRadar.gemini-catalog-drift: Updated 2026-07-20: the live `gemini_local` catalog still omits `gemini-3-flash-preview` and now also still advertises retired `gemini-2.0-flash*` IDs. The retirement half is routed separately in TSBC-1111.
openRadar.agy-gemini-routing-drift: Updated 2026-07-20: AGY 1.1.4 still accepts the tracked raw docs IDs and now also reroutes retired `gemini-2.0-flash*` IDs to Gemini 3.5 Flash instead of proving model-specific execution. Treat this as routing/name-surface drift until a harder probe proves model-specific execution on the Ireland lane.
openRadar.gemini-2.0-retirement-drift: NEW 2026-07-20: Google's official docs mark `gemini-2.0-flash` and `gemini-2.0-flash-lite` shut down as of June 1, 2026, but the live `gemini_local` catalog still advertises both IDs and AGY reroutes both to Gemini 3.5 Flash. Follow-up radar issue: TSBC-1111.
openRadar.claude-mythos-5: Unchanged 2026-07-20: docs and `claude_local` catalog still list it, but Claude Code still returns a model/no-access error on this lane; this still matches Anthropic's limited-availability positioning rather than a public GA flip.
openRadar.opencode-gpt-5.6-catalog: Unchanged 2026-07-20: `opencode_local` still reports 111 models and retains the tracked GPT-5.6 / gemma / Kimi notables including `cloudflare-ai-gateway/moonshotai/kimi-k3`. This remains served-catalog evidence from Ireland; a direct workspace `opencode` probe is still blocked locally by missing `CLOUDFLARE_GATEWAY_ID`.
openRadar.codex-gpt-5.6-base-id-gate: Unchanged 2026-07-20: the adapter catalog still lists `gpt-5.6` and the docs front-door still routes `gpt-5.6` to Sol, but the ChatGPT-backed Codex lane still rejects base `gpt-5.6` while `gpt-5.6-sol`, `gpt-5.6-terra`, and `gpt-5.6-luna` return OK. Keep explicit family IDs in matrices and smoke tests.

changesSincePrevious: Google retirement drift routed on Monday, July 20, 2026: official docs mark `gemini-2.0-flash` and `gemini-2.0-flash-lite` shut down, but `gemini_local` still advertises both IDs and AGY reroutes both to Gemini 3.5 Flash. Radar issue: TSBC-1111.
Monday local catalog observation recovered on July 20, 2026: Ollama responded again and shows `gemma3:12b` and `qwen3:8b` installed; no separate local-model alert opened.
